words: This manuscript is derived from a tape interview conducted by Sam Schrager
with Kate Sanderson Ualdron. The dialogue took place in the Waldron home in Bovill,
Idaho eighth, nineteen seventy-six.

speaker: KW
words: southern Idaho and gotten all of our furniture. And it had just come,

speaker: KW
words: it wasn't even unpacked. And I felt terribly bad about"it because I

speaker: KW
words: some of Ray's mother's furniture and it was antique. Marble topped tables

speaker: KW
words: and things like that. I was so happy to have those things. And one of the

speaker: KW
words: most beautiful dressers that I have ever seen. It was just mammoth. And

speaker: KW
words: the mirror was as long as that post down to there and had little drawers

speaker: KW
words: on each side of it. And just a real antique. It might have been Ray's

speaker: KW
words: grandmother's. But I was just keen about that. And the forest fire just

speaker: KW
words: got everything we had.

speaker: SAM
words: Was that all in Slabtown?

speaker: KW
words: It, well, I wasn't living in Slabtown, it was between where Byer live^ now.

speaker: KW
words: Then you go right by Byer's, there's a road, if you follow that road out

speaker: KW
words: you'd turn when you get up a ways. And there's where we had bought this

speaker: KW
words: little shack, two, three room shack, it really was. And we had moved it.

speaker: KW
words: We bought it from a man by the name of Fry. And we had moved it down there

speaker: KW
words: into that place.

speaker: SAM
words: You moved it from Slabtown. Was that Sam Fry that you bought it from?

speaker: KW
words: Yes. And we was going to have our, we kind of wanted to be by ourselves.

speaker: KW
words: Get out of Slabtown. I didn't live in Slabtown. I did one time but, we

speaker: KW
words: wanted to get down near mama, the home where Byer's and John live . We

speaker: KW
words: wanted to, and it was just about half way between there and Slabtown.

speaker: SAM
words: Can you tell me what happened that day, the way you remember it?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, land yes. You know, that fire had worried me for days. It was such

speaker: KW
words: a black, black smoke. And my father had told me,"Don't worry. That fire

speaker: KW
words: is so far away. It could never come over here. It'd have to come clear over

speaker: KW
words: that meadow." And he said,riYou haven't a thing in the world to worry about."

speaker: KW
words: But I worried any way. I was afraid of it. So I got up that morning, and

speaker: KW
words: dad reassured me again was so far away, it couldn't possibly come

speaker: KW
words: over there. Well, I went over to the neighbor's, there was a neighbor

speaker: KW
words: lived right across the road. And I said,"Aren't you afraid of that fire?"

speaker: KW
words: "Why no, Kate, that fire couldn't possibly come over here." So I went home

speaker: KW
words: and mama was ironing. And I was still worried about that fire. And within

speaker: KW
words: an hour's time, we were running for our lives. That fire, the wind come up

speaker: KW
words: and that fire come across that great meadow. Those miles and miles, just

speaker: KW
words: come a-flying. And the first thing I knew, a man came by in a, I don't

speaker: KW
words: know whether it was a, what he was driving. It was some kind of a car.

speaker: KW
words: I remember, or a pickup, I don't know what it was. And he grabbed both

speaker: KW
words: of my kids, and they were little. My little girl was about three and my little

speaker: KW
words: boy was about five. And he took, picked up my sister's baby. She was only

speaker: KW
words: about a year and a half,.two years old. And he said,"Now you people get

speaker: KW
words: out of here and get downtown." He said,"Because that fire's here,

speaker: KW
words: 'and it's dangerous." And I wouldn't leave until marna'd leave. And of course,

speaker: KW
words: mama, dad had told her, and she had some bonds'that she had there in the

speaker: KW
words: house. And she didn't know where dad had put those bonds. And she wasn't

speaker: KW
words: going to let those bonds burn up. And she just fooled around and fooled

speaker: KW
words: around. I said .^"Marna, we've got to get out of here." There was

speaker: KW
words: great balls of fire, oh, about that big around. And they just come through

speaker: KW
words: the air and light on a tree and that tree was just aflame. And the awfullest

speaker: KW
words: roar and the awfullest wind you ever saw, it was terrible. And that terrible,

speaker: KW
words: terrible fire. And mama come out the door. And my mother was. a woman that

speaker: KW
words: weighed about two hundred, she was a heavy woman, she wasn't like I am.

speaker: KW
words: I waSabout the weight I am now, I guess. And she come down the steps and

speaker: KW
words: I was just in front of her, I wouldn't go until she'd go and yet I'd run

speaker: KW
words: ahead of her a little ways. And she said afterwards, she said,"I don't know

speaker: KW
words: how you ever kept your feet." She said that wind was so terrible, that she

speaker: KW
words: said,"I couldn't hardly keep my feet." And how you ever kept your feet,

speaker: KW
words: she said, I don't know. And we got downtown to the depot.

speaker: SAM
words: Did you run all the way?

speaker: KW
words: Yes. Dust as hard as we could go. We ran. And the fire was breaking all

speaker: KW
words: around, when we got down to the depot the fire had come over the hiliL, and

speaker: KW
words: my brother stayed home, my brother John. And I don't know whether dad did

speaker: KW
words: or not. I don't remember, but I know my brother John did. And rny husband

speaker: KW
words: was out on the train, he was a fireman on one of the logging engines. And

speaker: KW
words: I could hear that engine. I knew every whistle on every shay.,that worked. .

speaker: KW
words: I knew their signals and everything. And near my husband blowing that whistle

speaker: KW
words: and calling for help. And you might know how that upset me. And said,"That's

speaker: KW
words: Ray, mother!" I said,"That's Ray!" and I said"They're calling for help."

speaker: KW
words: Of course, I was terribly upset. And...

speaker: SAM
words: That meant they were caught in the fire somewhere?

speaker: KW
words: Well,they were calling for help. They had come on the railroad as far as

speaker: KW
words: they could come and it was great trees on the track. They couldn't get by,

speaker: KW
words: and they were calling for help. I guess they got help, because, anyway, I

speaker: KW
words: got down to the depot. And of course, I was looking for my youngsters. And

speaker: KW
words: my I saw my sister's baby there and she'd been crying. And she was dirty. She'd

speaker: KW
words: gotten in the dirt and had rubbed it on her little face, and I didn't know

speaker: KW
words: know her. (laughs) And finally I said,"Fly land,Lela," I said,"that's your

speaker: KW
words: baby. That's your little girl, that di irty little girl there." And we were

speaker: KW
words: just horrified.

speaker: SAM
words: How had she gotten down there?

speaker: KW
words: She had run. She was down to the house and had warned us that the fire was

speaker: KW
words: coming over the meadow. And came down there to bring the news to us.

speaker: SAM
words: The little girl had?

speaker: KW
words: No, my sister, the mother of the little girl. And I found my two kids in

speaker: KW
words: the depot. They were all huddled around there, you know, in the depot,waiting for

speaker: KW
words: the flatcars. They had flatcars there and the people were

speaker: KW
words: climbing on the flatcars, 'cause the expected Bovill to go. And my brother

speaker: KW
words: John, in the little house thata he lives in, you know, it's out there,

speaker: KW
words: that fire come right down to that corner where I told you you'd go by to

speaker: KW
words: turn, and the wind carried the fire right up that way. Now, I don't know

speaker: KW
words: whether you're a Christian or not, or whether, but my brother John is a

speaker: KW
words: wonderful Christian man. And that was his home. And he was just married.

speaker: KW
words: And it was a brand new home, and he had beautiful furniture in it. And he

speaker: KW
words: just prayed. Kept praying. And asking God to protect his home. And that

speaker: KW
words: wind, that fire came right down to the corner of my father's land and it

speaker: KW
words: turned and went that way, and that saved Bovill and the whole country. Now

speaker: KW
words: some people would laugh at that, but that's just exactly what God did. He

speaker: KW
words: just turned that fire and it took off up over the hill. And it saved Bovill.

speaker: KW
words: And then we were on the flatcar going to Potlatch and...

speaker: SAM
words: Were there a lot of people?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, yes, all the people in Bovill. All the people had climbed on that flatcar

speaker: KW
words: 'cause ithey didn't know what that fire'd do. It was breaking all over the

speaker: KW
words: hills there. And I was on the flatcar. And I looked over at a pile of

speaker: KW
words: clothes over there and I said,"You know mother," I said,"that's looks like..."

speaker: KW
words: I had a brand new dress, brand new, peculiar shade of blue. And I said,

speaker: KW
words: "You know mother," I said,"That looks like^dross." And I said,"That other

speaker: KW
words: thing looks like my winter coat." And mama said,"Well go over and see."

speaker: KW
words: I went over there and my dress and I never have known where it came from.

speaker: KW
words: I never have known. I think somebody went by the house and saw those I had

speaker: KW
words: hanging there and just grabbed them and put them on their truck and dumped

speaker: KW
words: them on the car and thought the people had disowned them. And when we came

speaker: KW
words: home after they said the fire . gone over the hill. Bovill was saved,

speaker: KW
words: and we came home, I saw a stove sitting out in the meadow there as you

speaker: KW
words: come down that hill to come into Bovill, well that meadow over there, and

speaker: KW
words: I said to my mother, I said,"Mama!" I had a brand new range we had just

speaker: KW
words: bought it and I said,"That looks like my stove." And "Oh, no,"she said.

speaker: KW
words: "That wouldn't be your stove way down heEe." Well I said, it looks like

speaker: KW
words: my stove. And got out and went over there and it was my range. I don't

speaker: KW
words: know how it ever got there. Somebody just grabbed it out and set it over

speaker: KW
words: there where; no fire could get to it. And just took a chance. That's the

speaker: KW
words: way people did things. Oh, it was a sure funny experience. I've never

speaker: KW
words: never forgotten it, and I have nightmares over that fire. My dad had the

speaker: KW
words: awfullest time with me. I slept out on the front porch, and for two, three

speaker: KW
words: days, I had a nigtmare. And there'd be a tree that was still burning, you

speaker: KW
words: know. And it was at night the wind would burn it up, you know, and it

speaker: KW
words: would light up and I would rouse up in my sleep and I would see that and

speaker: KW
words: I would scream bloody murder. My dad slept upstairs, he'd come downstairs,

speaker: KW
words: "What's the matter." I said,"Everything's on fire again." I said,"Look at

speaker: KW
words: that tree." And dad would say,"Katie," he said,"that's just one tree, and

speaker: KW
words: it can't do a bit of harm." (laughs). Oh it was an awful experience. I was

speaker: KW
words: a wreck for weeks after that. Because we had lost everything we had and

speaker: KW
words: we had just sent to southern Idaho and shipped our furniture

speaker: KW
words: up here. And I had been up here all winter long and all summer and we nud

speaker: KW
words: just gotten along the best way we could, you know, and we sent for our

speaker: KW
words: furniture. And we bought that little house.

speaker: SAM
words: So the point where the fire turned from Bovill was the point at Johnny's

speaker: SAM
words: house?

speaker: KW
words: Dust right up at the turn of the road. It would have swept everything and

speaker: KW
words: come right down to Bovill if it hadn't a turned. It would have cleaned

speaker: KW
words: this place completely. Because that wind and those great balls of fire

speaker: KW
words: they just come sailing through the air. And they'd light in a tree or they'd

speaker: KW
words: light on a building. And dad and John fought them. They, those walls of

speaker: KW
words: fire were falling when mother and I left. But dad and my brother, John

speaker: KW
words: would put them out, would fight them. They stayed there, they didn't leave

speaker: KW
words: at all. And Ray would have .been there if he hadn't've been out there on

speaker: KW
words: the railroad.

speaker: SAM
words: How long did it take before you found out that Ray was alright?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, I don't remember, but it wasn't very long. The news at that time, you

speaker: KW
words: know, they made special efforts to let the people know.

speaker: SAM
words: Did you go all the way into Potlatch*

speaker: KW
words: Oh, yes. We went clear to Potlatch. All night, I don't remember if we

speaker: KW
words: stayed all next day or not. It's been so long ago,

speaker: SAM
words: So it upset you for weeks?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, yes. I was a wreck for days. And I was so discouraged because we had

speaker: KW
words: lost everything we had. And the house, and of course, I had lovely bedding.

speaker: KW
words: And to just lose everything, I was just... and I was young. I was only

speaker: KW
words: twenty-one, I guess.

speaker: SAM
words: How long had you been married at that time?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, I'd been married, three, four years. I had the two children, my little

speaker: KW
words: boy, my little girl was three years old, my baby and my little boy was

speaker: KW
words: about five. I've been married... I think if I remember right I'd been

speaker: KW
words: married about seven years. Between six and seven years. And...

speaker: SAM
words: What did you do then to start rebuilding again?

speaker: KW
words: Well, we spent the winter with mama at the house, see mama's house was,

speaker: KW
words: where Byer live, that's the old homeplace. It don't look like it did

speaker: KW
words: then. Terribly run down now. And I lived with mama all that winter. I had

speaker: KW
words: to. I had to. We didn't have no place, no home or anything. To keep house

speaker: KW
words: with or anything else. And then Ray was just a working man. And we lived

speaker: KW
words: with mama that winter. And then the next spring, why, we lived with mama

speaker: KW
words: I think, that next summer, too. And then we moved downtown here. That

speaker: KW
words: ended ou-T building a home out there. Well we did start us a log cabin out

speaker: KW
words: there, once too. Years later. I wanted a log cabin. And we decided we'd

speaker: KW
words: build us a log cabin out on the home place. But we gave that up

speaker: SAM

speaker: KW
words: Oh, yes. We never did recover some things. All my wedding gifts and my

speaker: KW
words: silverware, I recovered a lot of it and kept it as a souvenier. Because

speaker: KW
words: it was so that it had just melted, you know. I was thinking, I might

speaker: KW
words: have a piece of that, I might show it to you. I don't know.

speaker: SAM
words: the house did burn down though?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, yes, everything burned down but there was some things. Like that silverware,

speaker: KW
words: it was in a tub, a zinc tub and there was enough over it

speaker: KW
words: so it was just, the heat melted it. I think I have a couple spoons in the

speaker: KW
words: house here, I'll bring them out^She gets the spoons). Some of it I've got

speaker: KW
words: it's got great big gobs of silver that melted and just stuck up here. But

speaker: KW
words: it ruined it. It was brand new stuff. It was, i never had used it.

speaker: SAM
words: It's beautiful. Silver too.

speaker: KW
words: Yeah, it was Roger's silver and I had a lot of extra pieces, berry spoons

speaker: KW
words: and things like that that'.was with it. And I don't know where I put those.

speaker: KW
words: I think they're in an old trunk that I have.

speaker: KW
words: All the people in Slabtown did. Now some of those women, they buried their

speaker: KW
words: fruit. They had their fruit, it was in August you know, and they had their

speaker: KW
words: canned fruit. And in Slabtown, they buried their, and they, they saved their

speaker: KW
words: fruit. Canned fruit. Course, then canned fruit was lot harder to get up

speaker: KW
words: here then it is now cause we didn't have no cars and we just had to buy it

speaker: KW
words: down here at the store and can it, you know. Ancj it was really expensive.

speaker: SAM
words: But they didn't have warning either?

speaker: KW
words: No, they didn't. They did bury, people in town here, lot's of people, the

speaker: KW
words: old timers, I don't think any

speaker: KW
words: there are any of them left, Nell bmith might be

speaker: KW
words: one of them, that was here then.

speaker: SAM
words: I've spoken to her. he was here then.

speaker: KW
words: (Laughs) And you know, I went upstairs and packed a suitcase, and I thought

speaker: KW
words: of that when I thought of Nell. 'Cause Nell did the same identical thing with

speaker: KW
words: some of her clothes. She just made a mess out of everything. So didr.I. ^nd

speaker: KW
words: I grabbed a suitcase, I guess anything I'd see and I had my sister's grad

speaker: KW
words: uating underskirt. Mama made it with strips of insertion and little tucks,

speaker: KW
words: you know.And it was a beautiful lace skirt. I had that, in my suitcase.

speaker: KW
words: And I had, I had an evening slip, I had one of her slippers, just, .one.

speaker: KW
words: Didn't have the pair. I had one. And I had the awfullest mess in that suit

speaker: KW
words: case you just wouldn't think anybody that was, I wasn't sane. You

speaker: KW
words: I was just insane. At the things, anything I could see, I grabbed

speaker: KW
words: and shoved into that suitcase. I didn't have anything that amounted to

speaker: KW
words: anything.

speaker: SAM
words: I can understand, to have everything at once, going.

speaker: KW
words: And you just, you're in such a hurry to get it out and you'd want to net

speaker: KW
words: things that you prized, you know. Mama didn't prize nothing but those

speaker: KW
words: bonds, that's what she was after, what held her up.

speaker: KW
words: No. She realized that she had to let them burn and we started out for

words: town. The fire was breaking in all around and that wind was just, that

speaker: KW
words: wind carried you, you didn't have to run, just take a step, you know the

speaker: KW
words: wind was so bad. And, but the bonds didn't burn because the house didn't

speaker: KW
words: burn. And, but it was a sure funny trip. And the terrible thing. And I'm

speaker: KW
words: deathly afraid, that's the only thing I'm afraid of hers. We don't the snow

speaker: KW
words: and things like that don't bother me a bit, but Ilam deathly afraid of

speaker: KW
words: forest fires. I just scared to death of them. A pile of smoke coming up

speaker: KW
words: unexpectedly, even as long as been it jusijsends chills down my back.

speaker: KW
words: Cause I'm so afraid of them. But thank goodness we don't have any of their

speaker: KW
words: right around here. We haven'.t had. But you could never, never, never, it

speaker: KW
words: could be clear over there on those mountains and I'd want to leave and

speaker: KW
words: get out because you can't tell. My father been up here, an old man that

speaker: KW
words: had worked up here for years and years and years, and that neighbor was

speaker: KW
words: an elderly man,"Why that fire couldn't any more come across that meadow

speaker: KW
words: than it could fly." " think it was seven, eight miles away, I don't know

speaker: KW
words: know how far it was. Across that meadow. "You haven't a thing in the world

speaker: KW
words: to fear." And within an half hour or an hour, anyway, we were running for

speaker: KW
words: our lives. That very morning. And dad had warned me every morning, cause

speaker: KW
words: that fire had been going there for days. And it, smoke was so terrible, and

speaker: KW
words: the air was so heavy, and it just worried me to death. But, nobody thought,

speaker: KW
words: none of the people, none of the old people that knew about the fire, had

speaker: KW
words: worked in this country, thought that fire would ever do any damage. They

speaker: KW
words: had no idea of it. And that's why people weren't prepared, but there

speaker: KW
words: were people here in Bovill, a lot of them that buried their fine china

speaker: KW
words: and their silverware. They just didn't know. They were like I was, just

speaker: KW
words: didn't know. And I didn't think about doing anything like that, that wasn't

speaker: KW
words: what was bothering me. I wanted to get out with my kids. Get away.

speaker: SAM
words: I suppose that you'd heard the story that T.P. Jones had to let it burn.

speaker: KW
words: Yes. I donf.t know whether my brother, Byers told you or not but years and

speaker: KW
words: years later he was working on the CC and he had a crew of men out here in

speaker: KW
words: the mountains somewhere. And he had a general that was working there. He

speaker: KW
words: was one of the head men. He might have had a crow of men, boys, himself.

speaker: KW
words: And he come tearing into camp one night and he says,"What are we going to

speaker: KW
words: do?" He says,;,The whole country here is on fire!" And Byers says,"Well,

speaker: KW
words: where?" He says."Where's the fire?" And he says,"Well, come up here and

speaker: KW
words: seet" Byers went up the road a little ways, looked around, and he said,

speaker: KW
words: "Well, where's the fire?" And he said,"Well, can't you see it?" And, well

speaker: KW
words: Byers says, That's the sun set." He said,"Didn't you ever see a western

speaker: KW
words: sunset before?" He says,"I never saw a sunset like that in my life!"

speaker: KW
words: He says,"You're wrong." Well Byers just laughed. He said,"I am not wrong."

speaker: KW
words: He says,"Why, we have those sunsets all the time." The poor old general, he

speaker: KW
words: felt so silly, but he'd lived back there in New York where they can't see

speaker: KW
words: anything but the sky, you know. And do well, I guess, to see that. Because

speaker: KW
words: he had boys that had never, never, never, been able to sit and look like

speaker: KW
words: we do how at the heavens. They was raised right there in New York, you

speaker: KW
words: And they mnever could see anything.

speaker: SAM
words: Were you here when they had the fire the year before, the one that burned

speaker: SAM
words: town?

speaker: KW
words: Yes, I was living out at Slabtown at that time. When Bovill burned down?

speaker: KW
words: I was living out there then. I was just a little ways from the shop, you

speaker: KW
words: know. And Ray worked, when we first come^here, that's where we lived. Because

speaker: KW
words: Ray was a fireman and the trains all come into that shop there. My dad was

speaker: KW
words: foreman there. He had^seven hundred men under him. And they had a big payroll.

speaker: KW
words: They thought when Bovill first started, you know that it was going to be

speaker: KW
words: a marvelous town. had a fine hospital, we had banks, we had two fine

speaker: KW
words: dry goods stores, and two fine grocery stores, and there was a tavern and

speaker: KW
words: roaming houses, and a beautiful drugstore. And we had a doctor and two,

speaker: KW
words: three nurses. And the hospital, it was a well run hospital. And they thought

speaker: KW
words: that Bovill was going to be quite a village, quite a town. And, because

speaker: KW
words: Elk River's so far away. Hnd this was the location that they thought was ...

speaker: SAM
words: Did it boom?

speaker: KW
words: Oh, yes, it boomed. It boomed for a number of years. It boomed. And then

speaker: KW
words: they commenced gyppoing, that's what played havoc with everything. It's when

speaker: KW
words: they commenced gyppoing. It just went down and down and down. I don't think

speaker: KW
words: we even have a grocery store now. I guess there's a tavern that carries

speaker: KW
words: bread and a little milk.

speaker: SAM
words: Why did the gyppoing do that?

speaker: KW
words: Well, it did away with the railroad train, and it was cheaper for the Potlatch

speaker: KW
words: to let the,sod the trucks and the cars came in and it gave the m«n a better

speaker: KW
words: chance to make big money, and I guess it was cheaper for the Potlatch then

speaker: KW
words: for them to keep up their roads and their railroad tracks and their engines.

speaker: KW
